title: Noise and Hearing Conservation
subtitle: A 1-day HRD Corp-claimable safety programme for strengthening awareness and practice in noise and hearing conservation.
whyThisMatters: Noise and Hearing Conservation helps workplace teams recognize hazards, apply controls, and make noise and hearing conservation part of everyday operations. Participants explore introduction to sound and noise, Introduction To FMA (Noise Exposure Regulation) 1989 OSHA 1994, and hearing protectors and its application so safety expectations become easier to understand, discuss, and act on.
builtFor: Employees in operational, site-based, or technical roles
Supervisors responsible for daily safety practices
Teams that need stronger safety awareness and prevention habits
walkAwayWith: Recognize relevant workplace hazards and risk factors earlier.
Apply safer work practices and basic control measures.
Understand individual responsibility in safety, health, or environmental performance.
Support better reporting, prevention, and follow-through at work.

modules.title: Introduction To FMA (Noise Exposure Regulation) 1989 OSHA 1994

methodologyCaption: The workshop uses workplace examples, discussion, and practical hazard-recognition activities to make safe behavior concrete.
